summary: Repository README defines the package as skills-only and explicitly excludes AGENTS.md, system prompts, runtime prompts, and built-in prompt templates.
Package verifier gates top-level skill directories, skill frontmatter, routes, lifecycle, memory-system audit, skill-source audit, and commercial-content lint.

Commercial-content audit checks default files for owner-specific markers, scans all packaged skills for personal markers, and verifies the optional personal overlay preserves owner identity without scanning removed prompt templates.
Lifecycle audit blocks accepted snapshot writes when unallowlisted suspicious findings are present and accepts clean snapshot refreshes only after scan review.
Memory context resolver reports projectless global preference requests as global writes while keeping explicit project memory routed to project docs.
Global capture writer now writes Candidate rows using the Candidate Preferences schema and creates a clean Change Log section for new profiles.
Project memory audit verifies evidence source hashes and requires docs index coverage for Markdown, JSON, and JSONL durable memory files.
Windows installer requires -Force for owner-specific overlays, backs up destinations before replacement, and skips top-level directories missing SKILL.md.
Skill-source audit requires inventory coverage, source-review, commercial-use, and redistribution metadata for every packaged skill.
Commercial release builder creates a distribution directory that excludes owner-specific overlays and records that built-in prompts are not included.
Skills inventory records source review, commercial-use status, and manual redistribution review status for all 143 packaged skills.
Project memory initializer provides the conversational "初始化本项目" entrypoint by running pre-audit, safe initialization, retrieval summary write, and final audit.
macOS installer copies packaged skills into the selected Codex home, backs up replaced skills with --force, supports explicit overlays, and skips non-skill directories.
Rule-governor guidance now treats this repository as a skills-only distribution and keeps prompt templates outside the bundle unless distribution rules change.
